title: Dharmesvara Temple
tagline: Dharmesvara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Dharmesvara Temple, located in Kondrahalli, Bangalore Rural, Hoskote Taluk in Bangalore, Karnataka, India, is dedicated to the deity Dharmesvara (the Hindu god Shiva).
overview.description: Dharmesvara Temple, located in Kondrahalli, Bangalore Rural, Hoskote Taluk in Bangalore, Karnataka, India, is dedicated to the deity Dharmesvara (the Hindu god Shiva). It dates back to the Chola period of 1065 AD. The temple also has 5000 years of history as informed by the temple in charge, Mr. Manjunath. Yaksha kandam of Mahabharata is believed to have taken place on this site.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dharmesvara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dharmesvara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dharmesvara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (24 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dharmesvara Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dharmesvara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dharmesvara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Dharmesvara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dharmesvara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Agasarahalli, Nelamangala, Guttahalli, Kolar West, Shivanapur, Agrahara Vaddahalli, Agrahara, Channarayapatna.
